questions.question: Two identical containers are filled with hot water at $90^\circ\text{C}$. Container A is painted dull black and Container B is polished silver. Which container will cool down to room temperature more quickly?
options: Container A, because it is a better emitter of radiation.
Container B, because it is a better reflector of radiation.
Container A, because it absorbs 'cold' from the room faster.
Both will cool at the same rate if they are in the same room.
questions.explanation:
Dull black surfaces are not only good absorbers but also excellent emitters of infrared radiation. 

**Common mistake:** Thinking that black only relates to absorption and not emission.

questions.question: How does the surface temperature of an object affect its rate of radiation emission?
options: The higher the surface temperature, the higher the rate of emission.
The higher the surface temperature, the lower the rate of emission.
Temperature does not affect the rate of radiation emission.
Emission only occurs once a specific threshold temperature is reached.
questions.explanation:
The rate of emission of radiation depends directly on the surface temperature of the object. 

**Common mistake:** Thinking the color is more important than the temperature for the rate of energy loss.

questions.question: A student wears dark-colored clothes on a very hot, sunny day. Why do they feel hotter than someone wearing white clothes?
options: Dark colors are better absorbers of infrared radiation from the Sun.
Dark colors are better conductors of heat from the air.
Dark colors prevent the body from emitting its own radiation.
Dark colors create their own thermal energy when exposed to light.
questions.explanation:
Dark-colored clothes absorb a higher percentage of the Sun's incident infrared radiation. 

**Common mistake:** Attributing the heat gain to conduction from the air rather than radiation from the Sun.

questions.question: Which of the following factors would NOT increase the rate at which an object emits radiation?
options: Decreasing the surface area of the object.
Increasing the surface temperature of the object.
Painting the object dull black.
Increasing the surface area of the object.
questions.explanation:
Decreasing surface area reduces the amount of space available for radiation to escape, thus decreasing the rate. 

**Common mistake:** Thinking a smaller, 'concentrated' surface emits faster.

questions.question: In an experiment comparing the absorption of heat by two surfaces, two thermometers are placed at equal distances from a heater. One is covered in black foil and the other in silver foil. What will be observed?
options: The black foil thermometer shows a faster temperature rise.
The silver foil thermometer shows a faster temperature rise.
Both thermometers will show the same temperature rise.
The silver foil thermometer will show a temperature decrease.
questions.explanation:
The black surface absorbs more infrared radiation per second than the silver surface, leading to a faster temperature increase. 

**Common mistake:** Thinking the silver foil 'focuses' the heat into the thermometer.
